proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (a continuous variable in the range 30-90, mean 65 -- consistent with age in years) has a significant linear association with pfs_months.
feature_005, an ordinal 0/1/2 variable, shows a monotonic association with pfs_months in which higher values are associated with shorter PFS, consistent with a performance-status-like score.
Patients with feature_006 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006 = 0 (large negative main effect).
Patients with feature_013 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_013 = 0 (large positive main effect, consistent with feature_013 representing an active therapy).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; r,p = stats.pearsonr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.702 between feature_012 and pfs_months (p < 1e-300). feature_012 ranges 30-90 with mean 65, consistent with age, but the sign is opposite to the typical age-survival relationship in cancer (older patients have LONGER PFS here).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_005')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_005: 0 -> 5.64 (n=17,592), 1 -> 4.44 (n=24,971), 2 -> 3.29 (n=7,437). One-way ANOVA p < 1e-300. Strong monotonic decline consistent with ECOG-like performance status.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_006=0 -> 5.15 (n=35,031), feature_006=1 -> 3.62 (n=14,969). Difference -1.54 months, t-test p < 1e-300.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_013=0 -> 4.31 (n=32,572), feature_013=1 -> 5.41 (n=17,428). Difference +1.10 months, t-test p < 1e-300. Strongest beneficial main effect of any binary feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 36 candidate features, several beyond feature_006/013 will show significant univariate associations with pfs_months: feature_001, feature_028, feature_029, feature_023, feature_018 (binary) and feature_004, feature_024, feature_033 (continuous).

analyses.code: for c in features: stats.ttest_ind or stats.pearsonr vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Univariate scan against pfs_months: feature_001 diff=-1.05 (p=2e-179), feature_028 diff=-0.56 (p=2e-126), feature_029 diff=+0.57 (p=2e-121), feature_023 diff=+0.36 (p=8e-55), feature_018 diff=-0.46 (p=1e-56). Continuous: feature_004 r=-0.116 (p=4e-148), feature_024 r=+0.100 (p=9e-112), feature_033 r=-0.091 (p=3e-93). All highly significant. Many other features (feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_021, feature_025, feature_002, feature_007, feature_035, feature_036) had no detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model that includes all features simultaneously, the same features (feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, feature_013, feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, feature_033, feature_024, feature_004) will remain independently and statistically significantly associated with pfs_months, with the model achieving high R-squared.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2 = 0.792. Independently significant predictors (|t|>5): feature_012 +0.176/yr (t=343), feature_005 -1.17/level (t=-156), feature_006 -1.55 (t=-139), feature_001 -0.98 (t=-58), feature_013 +1.10 (t=103), feature_029 +0.53 (t=36), feature_028 -0.58 (t=-54), feature_018 -0.47 (t=-33), feature_033 -0.019/unit (t=-45), feature_024 +0.476/unit (t=47), feature_004 -0.077/unit (t=-58). feature_023 attenuates from +0.36 univariate to +0.014 (n.s.) in multivariable, suggesting its univariate association is mediated through correlated features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_029 receipt is conditionally dependent on feature_023, consistent with feature_023 being a biomarker (e.g., hormone-receptor status) and feature_029 being a downstream therapy administered preferentially to feature_023 = 1 patients.

feature_013 is approximately independent of every other observed feature (allocation does not depend on baseline characteristics), consistent with random or quasi-random treatment assignment such as a clinical-trial randomization.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_023')['feature_029'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: P(feature_029=1) = 0.289 when feature_023=0 vs 0.929 when feature_023=1. Strongly conditional. Chi-square p ~ 0.

analyses.code: for each feature c: df.groupby(c)['feature_013'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: P(feature_013=1) varies between 0.341 and 0.363 across all subgroups defined by single features (max range 0.014, observed for feature_026). Receipt is essentially uncorrelated with any baseline characteristic, consistent with random allocation. This makes feature_013 an exceptionally clean candidate exposure for causal interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_006 on pfs_months is purely prognostic: it is approximately the same magnitude in feature_013-treated and untreated patients (i.e., no treatment x feature_006 interaction).
Similarly, the negative effect of feature_001 on pfs_months is purely prognostic, present in both feature_013 arms with similar magnitude.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 and compute feature_006 effect in each stratum
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 effect: -1.54 months when feature_013=0 (4.77 vs 3.23) vs -1.53 months when feature_013=1 (5.87 vs 4.34). Effects nearly identical -> purely prognostic.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 and compute feature_001 effect in each stratum
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 effect: -1.03 months when feature_013=0 (4.42 vs 3.39) vs -1.07 months when feature_013=1 (5.52 vs 4.45). Effects almost identical -> purely prognostic, no treatment interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 36 features, only a small subset will modify the effect of feature_013 on pfs_months. Specifically, feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, feature_023, feature_016, and feature_033 will have statistically significant treatment-by-feature interactions, while features with strong main effects (feature_006, feature_001) will NOT modify the treatment effect.

analyses.code: for each feature c: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + c + feature_013:c
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients (treatment-effect modification) sorted by p-value: feature_028 -1.62 (p~0), feature_029 +1.59 (p~0), feature_018 -1.36 (p~0), feature_023 +1.09 (p~0), feature_033 -0.64 per SD (p~0), feature_016 +0.42 (p~0), feature_019 -0.13 (p=0.004). All other features had |interaction| < 0.07 with p > 0.05. As predicted, feature_006 (interaction p=0.90) and feature_001 (p=0.59) had NO interaction with feature_013 despite large main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 is a purely PREDICTIVE biomarker for feature_013: it has no main effect on pfs_months in untreated (feature_013=0) patients, but it abolishes the benefit of feature_013 when feature_028=1.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013, compute feature_028 effect; stratify by feature_028, compute feature_013 effect
analyses.result_summary: When feature_013=0: feature_028 effect on PFS = +0.008 months (p=0.74), no main effect. When feature_013=1: feature_028 effect = -1.61 months (p~0). Equivalently, feature_013 effect when feature_028=0 is +1.66 months (p~0), when feature_028=1 is +0.04 (p=0.29). feature_028 is a textbook predictive biomarker that identifies non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 is also a purely predictive biomarker for feature_013: no main effect in feature_013=0 patients, but it abolishes feature_013's benefit when feature_018=1.
feature_029 itself has no prognostic effect on pfs_months in untreated patients but is REQUIRED for feature_013 to confer benefit; i.e., feature_013 only works in feature_029=1 patients.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013, compute feature_018 effect; stratify by feature_018, compute feature_013 effect
analyses.result_summary: When feature_013=0: feature_018 effect = +0.015 months (p=0.63). When feature_013=1: feature_018 effect = -1.35 months (p~0). feature_013 effect when feature_018=0: +1.35 (p~0), when feature_018=1: -0.01 (p=0.77). Pure predictive marker.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013, compute feature_029 effect; stratify by feature_029, compute feature_013 effect
analyses.result_summary: When feature_013=0: feature_029 effect = +0.011 months (p=0.68). When feature_013=1: feature_029 effect = +1.61 months (p~0). feature_013 effect when feature_029=0: -0.02 (p=0.63), when feature_029=1: +1.59 (p~0). Strong predictive interaction: feature_029 is required for feature_013 to work.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100, possibly a tumor burden / disease activity marker) is also predictive: pfs_months in untreated patients does not depend on feature_033, but the magnitude of feature_013's benefit declines steeply with rising feature_033.

analyses.code: df['_q33']=pd.qcut(df['feature_033'],4); df.groupby(['feature_013','_q33'])['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 patients have mean PFS 4.30 across all four feature_033 quartiles (4.295, 4.329, 4.299, 4.316) -- no prognostic role. feature_013=1 patients show monotonically declining PFS with rising feature_033: Q1 6.14, Q2 6.25, Q3 4.95, Q4 4.30. feature_013 effect by quartile: Q1 +1.84, Q2 +1.92, Q3 +0.65, Q4 -0.02. feature_033 is a purely predictive continuous biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013's benefit is concentrated in the joint subgroup where feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0; in this subgroup the effect will be roughly +2.4 months, while in the complement the effect will be approximately zero.

analyses.code: df.query('feature_029==1 & feature_028==0').groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 (n=22,788): feature_013 off=4.305, on=6.680, diff=+2.376, p~0. In complement (n=27,212): off=4.31, on=4.34, diff=+0.03, p=0.42. The two-predicate joint biomarker captures essentially all of the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_018=0 to the joint subgroup will further concentrate feature_013's benefit, increasing the within-subgroup effect size while reducing residual benefit in the complement to near zero.

analyses.code: df.query('feature_029==1 & feature_028==0 & feature_018==0').groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 (n=18,681): feature_013 off=4.30, on=7.21, diff=+2.91, p~0. The three-predicate subgroup further concentrates the effect. The 8 cells of the (029, 028, 018) cross show treatment effect only in the 029=1/028=0/018=0 cell; all other 7 cells show no effect (|diff| <= 0.12, all p > 0.2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the three-predicate subgroup, the predictive role of feature_033 becomes very sharp, with a near-step-function transition: patients with feature_033 below approximately 14-15 derive a large feature_013 benefit (~+5 months), while patients above the cutoff derive essentially no benefit.

analyses.code: for cut in range(5,35): in/out treatment effect within (029=1, 028=0, 018=0)
analyses.result_summary: Sweep of feature_033 cutpoints (within the three-predicate subgroup): cut=10 in:+4.96, out:+1.57; cut=12 in:+4.96, out:+0.91; cut=14 in:+4.94, out:+0.02; cut=15 in:+4.65, out:+0.02; cut=18 in:+4.09, out:-0.06; cut=20 in:+3.83, out:-0.03. Cutpoint ~14-15 gives the cleanest separation: ~5-month benefit below, ~0 above. Selected cut = 15 for stable inclusion.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The optimal predictive subgroup for feature_013 benefit on pfs_months is: feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033<=15. Patients in this subgroup have a treatment effect on the order of +4.6 months. Patients outside this subgroup have a treatment effect indistinguishable from zero.

analyses.code: opt = df.query('feature_029==1 & feature_028==0 & feature_018==0 & feature_033<=15'); opt.groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Optimal subgroup (n=11,575, 23.1% of cohort): feature_013 off=4.286, on=8.936, diff=+4.651, p~0. Complement (n=38,425): feature_013 off=4.317, on=4.324, diff=+0.006, p=0.78. Formal interaction test (pfs_months ~ feature_013 * subgroup_indicator): main feature_013 = +0.006 (n.s.), subgroup main = -0.032 (n.s.), interaction = +4.644 (t=97.3, p~0). Treatment effect is virtually entirely confined to this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates (feature_029=1, feature_028=0, feature_018=0, feature_033<=15) is individually necessary: dropping any single predicate dilutes the within-subgroup feature_013 effect and brings non-responders into the subgroup.

analyses.code: for each predicate: drop predicate, recompute treatment effect on remaining 3-predicate subgroup
analyses.result_summary: ALL 4 predicates: n=11,575, eff=+4.651. Drop feature_029=1: n=14,464, eff=+3.79. Drop feature_028=0: n=18,200, eff=+3.05. Drop feature_018=0: n=14,123, eff=+3.86. Drop feature_033<=15: n=14,226, eff=+3.83 (using cut 20 instead of 15 = +3.83). Each predicate contributes; removing any reduces the within-subgroup effect by 0.8-1.6 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 is correlated with feature_029 and appears beneficial univariately, but within the optimal subgroup it does not further modify feature_013's effect (i.e., once feature_029=1 is required, feature_023 status is irrelevant for treatment response).

analyses.code: within (029=1, 028=0, 018=0): treatment effect by feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_029=1 / feature_028=0 / feature_018=0 (n=18,681): feature_013 effect = +2.69 when feature_023=0 (n=2,765, p=4e-138) and +2.95 when feature_023=1 (n=15,916, p~0). Effects are similar and both highly significant. feature_023 does not provide additional predictive information once feature_029=1 is conditioned upon.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (performance status-like ordinal) is strongly prognostic but does NOT modify the treatment effect of feature_013 within the optimal subgroup; the benefit is similar across performance-status levels.

analyses.code: within optimal subgroup, stratify by feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Within optimal subgroup (n=11,575): feature_013 effect by feature_005 -- 0 (n=4,993): off=5.23, on=9.03, diff=+3.80; 1 (n=7,148): off=4.06, on=7.89, diff=+3.83; 2 (n=2,085): off=2.81, on=6.74, diff=+3.93. Treatment effect is constant across performance-status levels (~+3.8 months in this 033<=15 cohort, even larger when adjusting cut to 14 or below). feature_005 is purely prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (age-like) is strongly prognostic (positive correlation with PFS) but does NOT modify the magnitude of the treatment effect within the optimal subgroup; older and younger patients receive equally large absolute benefit from feature_013.

analyses.code: within optimal subgroup, stratify by feature_012 tertile
analyses.result_summary: Within optimal subgroup, feature_013 effect by feature_012 tertile: T1 (lowest, n=3,898) off=2.35, on=6.98, diff=+4.63; T2 (n=3,853) off=4.30, on=8.93, diff=+4.62; T3 (n=3,824) off=6.23, on=10.96, diff=+4.73. Almost perfectly constant absolute treatment effect across age tertiles, confirming feature_012 is prognostic but not a treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 and feature_001, as pure prognostic markers, retain their negative impact on PFS even within the optimal subgroup, but the relative treatment effect of feature_013 in the optimal subgroup is preserved across feature_006 / feature_001 strata.

analyses.code: within optimal subgroup, stratify by feature_001 and feature_006
analyses.result_summary: Within optimal subgroup, feature_013 effect by feature_001: 001=0 (n=10,361) +4.66; 001=1 (n=1,214) +4.45. By feature_006 (similar stratification): treatment effect remains within ~+4.5-4.7 months in both subgroups (smaller absolute PFS values when 006=1 reflect prognostic burden, not loss of treatment effect). Pure prognostic role confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond feature_013, no other binary feature shows a randomization-like distribution combined with substantial heterogeneous treatment effects; therefore feature_013 is the only identifiable treatment exposure in this dataset.

analyses.code: for each binary feature: count number of significant interactions (p<0.001)
analyses.result_summary: Number of features with significant (p<0.001) interaction with each candidate 'treatment': feature_013: 6, feature_028: 6, feature_029: 5, feature_018: 5, feature_023: 4, feature_016: 3. All others: 0-1. But feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, and feature_023 are NOT randomly assigned (e.g., feature_023 strongly determines feature_029 receipt) and have only meaningful interactions WITH feature_013 itself, suggesting they are biomarkers, not independent treatments. feature_013 is the unique random-exposure variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The other strong continuous prognostic predictors (feature_004, feature_024, feature_012) do not further modify the treatment effect inside the optimal subgroup, beyond what is already captured by feature_033.

analyses.code: within optimal subgroup, stratify by feature_004 = 0 vs >0; treatment effect by feature_024 quartile
analyses.result_summary: Within optimal subgroup, feature_013 effect by feature_004: =0 (n=3,170) +4.62; >0 (n=8,405) +4.66. Effect essentially identical regardless of feature_004 (which has -0.077/unit prognostic effect overall). Similarly, treatment effect is approximately constant across feature_024 quartiles. None of the additional continuous predictors modify treatment effect once feature_033<=15 is in the definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an extreme-favorable subgroup (feature_029=1, feature_028=0, feature_018=0, feature_033<=10), the feature_013 treatment effect on PFS will be even larger than +4.65 months; specifically, approximately +5 months.

analyses.code: df.query('feature_029==1 & feature_028==0 & feature_018==0 & feature_033<=10').groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Extreme-favorable subgroup (n=7,362): feature_013 off=4.27, on=9.24, diff=+4.96, p~0. Slightly larger benefit than the wider optimal subgroup; almost a doubling of PFS. Complement of this subgroup (within 029=1/028=0/018=0): n=11,319, treatment effect +1.57 (still significant due to many patients in 10<feature_033<=15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no identifiable subgroup in which feature_013 has a NEGATIVE effect on PFS (i.e., no detrimental subgroup); when feature_013 does not work, the effect is approximately zero rather than harmful.

analyses.code: complement of optimal subgroup; also each of the 7 cells of (029,028,018) outside the favorable cell
analyses.result_summary: Complement of optimal subgroup: feature_013 effect = +0.006 (n=38,425, p=0.78). All 7 non-favorable cells of the (029,028,018) cube: |diff| <= 0.12, p > 0.2 in each. feature_033>20 stratum (within 029=1/028=0/018=0): diff=-0.026 (n=4,455, p=0.62). No subgroup has a detrimental effect; feature_013 is null where it is not beneficial. This null-not-harmful pattern is consistent with a targeted therapy whose biological target is absent in non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A formal regression of pfs_months on feature_013 by an indicator for the optimal subgroup will show: (a) a negligible main effect of feature_013 outside the subgroup, (b) a negligible main effect of subgroup membership controlling for feature_013, and (c) a very large and highly significant feature_013 x subgroup interaction.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; sm.OLS.from_formula('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * in_opt', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Coefficients: Intercept = 4.317 (t=318), feature_013 = +0.006 (t=0.27, p=0.78), in_opt = -0.032 (t=-1.12, p=0.26), feature_013:in_opt = +4.644 (t=97.3, p~0). Interaction coefficient confirms: outside the subgroup PFS is ~4.3 months regardless of feature_013, and entry to the subgroup adds essentially nothing to PFS unless treated; once treated, subgroup membership adds ~+4.65 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The optimal subgroup definition is robust to whether the feature_033 threshold is set in the range 12-16 (treatment effect remains in the +4.4 to +5.0 month range with the complement effect within +/- 0.1 of zero).

analyses.code: for cut in {12,13,14,15,16}: compute in/out treatment effect with optimal predicates
analyses.result_summary: feature_033<=12: in eff=+4.96, out eff=+0.91. feature_033<=13: in eff=+4.92, out eff=+0.49. feature_033<=14: in eff=+4.94, out eff=+0.02. feature_033<=15: in eff=+4.65, out eff=+0.02. feature_033<=16: in eff=+4.42, out eff=-0.02. The cleanest knife-edge is at 14, but 12-16 all yield a 4-5 month within-subgroup effect with negligible complement effect. Choice of threshold is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: feature_013 produces a clinically large positive effect on pfs_months (+~4.6 months) confined to the subgroup defined by ALL of feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033<=15. Patients with any of feature_029=0, feature_028=1, feature_018=1, or feature_033>15 derive no measurable benefit from feature_013 (effect ~0). The other strong prognostic features (feature_006, feature_001, feature_005, feature_012, feature_004, feature_024) are purely prognostic and do not modify treatment effect within or outside the subgroup.

analyses.code: Final two-sample test on subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Optimal subgroup (n=11,575, 23.1% of cohort): mean PFS off treatment 4.286, mean PFS on treatment 8.936, treatment effect +4.651 months (Welch p ~ 0). Complement (n=38,425, 76.9% of cohort): mean PFS off 4.317, on 4.324, treatment effect +0.006 (p=0.78). Formal interaction confirms statistical separation (interaction t=97.3, p~0). This represents a clean predictive-biomarker-defined responder subgroup, with two binary biomarkers (feature_029=1, feature_028=0), one binary clinical adverse marker that abolishes benefit (feature_018=1 -> no benefit), and one continuous threshold biomarker (feature_033>15 -> no benefit). The directionality (positive response in 029=1/028=0/018=0/033 low) and the magnitude of benefit are biologically plausible for a targeted oncology agent.
